sa_verse: तपः स्वाध्यायनिरतं तपस्वी वाग्विदां वरम्। नारदं परिपप्रच्छ वाल्मीकिर्मुनिपुङ्गवम्॥ तपःस्वाध्यायनिरतं (tapaḥsvādhyāyanirataṃ) तपः (tapaḥ) + स्वाध्याय (svādhyāya) + निरतम् (niratam)
en: The ascetic Vālmīki asked Nārada, the best of sages and foremost of those conversant with words, ever engaged in austerities and Vedic studies.

sa_verse: को न्वस्मिन् साम्प्रतं लोке गुणवान् कश्च वीर्यवान्। धर्मज्ञश्च कृतज्ञश्च सत्यवाक्यो दृढव्रतः॥ को न्वस्मिन् (ko nvasmin) कः नु अस्मिन् (kaḥ nu asmin)
en: Who, at present in this world, is truly virtuous and vigorous, a knower of dharma and grateful, truthful in speech, and firm in his vows?

sa_verse: चारित्रेण च को युक्तः सर्वभूतेषु को हितः। विद्वान् कः कः समर्थश्च कश्चैकप्रियदर्शनः॥ चारित्रेण च (cāritreṇa ca) चारित्र (cāritra) + च (ca)
en: Who is endowed with noble character, benevolent to all beings, who is learned and capable, and who is uniquely pleasant to behold?

sa_verse: आत्मवान् को जितक्रोधो द्युतिमान् कोऽनसूयकः। कस्य बिभ्यति देवाश्च जातरोषस्य संयुगे॥ आत्मवान् को (ātmavān ko) आत्मवत् (ātmavat) + कः (kaḥ)
en: Who is self-possessed, has conquered anger, is brilliant, and free from envy? And of whom, when his anger is aroused in battle, are even the gods afraid?

sa_verse: एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं परं कौतूहलं हि मे। महर्षे त्वं समर्थोऽसि ज्ञातुमेवंविधं नरम्॥ एतद् इच्छामि अहं श्रोतुम् (etad icchāmi ahaṃ śrotum) एतत् (etat) + इष् (iṣ, root: to wish) + अहम् (aham) + श्रु (śru, root: to hear)
en: This I wish to hear, for my curiosity is indeed immense. O great sage, you are capable of knowing such a man.
Who is learned, capable, and pleasing to behold
Who is self-controlled, has conquered anger, is radiant and free from envy
Of whom do even the gods fear when he becomes angry in battle
